f257528534291f3a484759d114cc4d58.location: RheinMain Congress Center, Wiesbaden, Frankfurt RheinMain region (onsite and online)
f257528534291f3a484759d114cc4d58.description: The Service Design Global Conference 2026 (SDGC26) takes place 28-30 October in the Frankfurt RheinMain region, both onsite and online. It aims to discover how service design drives innovation and transforms modern organizations. The theme 'Beyond Boundaries: Enabling Solutions That Shape Tomorrow' explores how organizations across public and private sectors and the communities they serve can move past today’s limits to shape more resilient, meaningful futures through service design. SDGC26 brings together pioneers and practitioners for breakthrough ideas, practical tools, and meaningful connections, engaging with leading voices and exploring cutting-edge approaches to propel organizations forward.

a145bf6f8fe0ddc5c8daaece479e6f79.description: The Service Design Global Conference 2026 (SDGC26) will take place from 28-30 October 2026, gathering the international Service Design community both in-person in Wiesbaden, in the vibrant Frankfurt RheinMain region, and online for virtual attendees. The conference is organized in cooperation with the World Design Capital 2026. The 3-day format includes workshops on 28 October (requiring separate purchase) followed by two full days of inspiring conference talks, keynotes, and panels on 29-30 October. In-person attendees will meet at the state-of-the-art RheinMain CongressCenter (RMCC).

6d1d884dc2cde9af420e29001b5507e9.location: RheinMain Congress Center, Friedrich-Ebert-Allee 1, 65185 Wiesbaden, Germany
6d1d884dc2cde9af420e29001b5507e9.description: The Service Design Global Conference 2026 (SDGC26) is being held from October 28-30, 2026, at the RheinMain Congress Center in Wiesbaden, Germany. Early Bird tickets are available until June 30, 2026, offering savings for both in-person and virtual attendees. In-person tickets grant access to the conference on October 29-30, with an optional add-on for a closing event. Virtual tickets provide online access for the same dates, including a special rate for students. Workshops scheduled for October 28 are not included in these ticket types.

b07d5a049880291e09b7d639cd19fd89.description: The Service Design Network has announced that registration for the Service Design Global Conference 2026 (SDGC26) is officially open. The event brings the international Service Design community to Germany, with in-person attendance in Wiesbaden, in the vibrant Frankfurt RheinMain region, and online for virtual attendees. It is organized in cooperation with the World Design Capital Frankfurt RheinMain 2026.

f5f0c8739b6f46c12b0b054c0709fc9c.summary: UX Discussion: How Junior Designers Can Stand Out

bac8f4c690ad5025d5fc73d0c23cee4e.description: The prompt:UX Conference focuses on the intersection of artificial intelligence and user experience design, gathering designers, researchers, and product managers to explore how AI is reshaping UX and how to prepare strategies, tools, and workflows accordingly. Expect engaging keynotes from industry leaders, hands-on workshops, case studies, and a dedicated networking reception, all aimed at helping your team integrate AI into UX in meaningful, future-ready ways. If you’re looking to stay at the forefront of AI-powered UX design and research, this conference offers the insights, practical learning, and connections to make that happen.

843537c576e591036fadef7b0c7fc835.location: Caesars Palace Las Vegas Hotel and Casino, 3570 S Las Vegas Blvd, Las Vegas, NV 89109
843537c576e591036fadef7b0c7fc835.description: Meet up with fellow UX professionals from around the world for four days of amazing content and camaraderie! A Monday of full and half-day workshops followed by three days of keynotes, panels, sessions, and networking opportunities during the full conference.

ed3534257860f9a4738515fa10d79140.location: University of Ottawa & Ottawa Conference & Event Centre, Ottawa
ed3534257860f9a4738515fa10d79140.description: Innovate to elevate: Unlocking the path to progressive governance As we move into 2026, public sector transformation and innovation remain crucial for shaping responsive and resilient governance. The evolution from Gov 2.0's digital services to Gov 3.0's intelligent, integrated systems offers governments unprecedented opportunities to enhance service delivery, improve efficiency, and foster greater civic engagement—aligning with the federal government's commitment to digital government and service excellence. By embracing smart technologies, data analytics, and AI, public administrations can do more with less, boost productivity, and ensure policies are more adaptive to citizens' evolving needs through personalized, proactive governance. This transformation involves cultivating a culture of continuous improvement and innovation within public entities, maximizing impact with constrained resources.Government Innovation Week Federal brings together Federal leaders to integrate Gov 3.0 innovations while safeguarding equity, privacy, and security—core priorities of the current government's mandate. Join us to explore strategies for navigating digital transformation complexities, enhancing operational productivity, and prioritizing accessibility to bridge the digital divide, supporting the government's commitment to inclusive digital services. Register now to connect with experts from Public Safety Canada, Export Development Canada, Service Canada, the Department of National Defence, and more, engage in meaningful discussions, and be part of the movement setting a new benchmark for governance in the digital age.

30b844e64825ebe0956d5bc2269a6868.summary: OECD Call for Government Innovations 2026 Edition
30b844e64825ebe0956d5bc2269a6868.description: The 8th OECD Call for Government Innovations invites global submissions from public servants and those working closely with the public sector. The call aims to identify and share new, implemented, and impactful innovations, particularly across themes like simplifying government, citizen-government relationships, and measuring efficiency/effectiveness. Selected cases will be published in the OPSI Case Study Library and the 2026 OECD report, with outstanding shortlisted cases invited to a global showcase event in Paris.

d490fa44810f5a0f7c4cf528c59fecaf.description: This report provides a government-specific take on Deloitte’s Tech Trends 2026, spotlighting the accelerating technology trends most likely to disrupt enterprise IT over the next 18 to 24 months. It explores innovations most relevant for governments and assesses their readiness to scale, focusing on how public sector organizations can harness emerging technologies, especially AI, to redesign processes, align investments with mission objectives, and transform operations for the future. Key topics include 'AI goes physical: Navigating the convergence of AI and robotics' and 'The agentic reality check: Preparing for a silicon-based workforce'.

4dafba861b5a11b9de0b979f1ea6a27f.summary: Pre-Summit Event for 2026 AI Impact Summit
4dafba861b5a11b9de0b979f1ea6a27f.description: An event serving as a prelude to the main 2026 AI Impact Summit, for which a recap was published on February 13, 2026.

d37c77bede552abef85ba2c771cd3139.summary: SDN Global Café - From evidence to action
d37c77bede552abef85ba2c771cd3139.description: From Evidence to Action: Join the SDN Global Café Exploring the UK’s First Public Design Evidence Review with Professor Lucy Kimbell and David Singer.

2124416eb82b04c2bba637b9cc448bdd.summary: Inspire & Connect at UXCON26, where UX professionals, product managers, and engineers collaborate to design better experiences.
2124416eb82b04c2bba637b9cc448bdd.description: A day of bold ideas, real connection, and unforgettable experiences. UXCON26 brings together leaders in UX, product management, and engineering to explore true collaboration in shaping better experiences. It aims to reignite conversations about the essence of the UX craft and how professionals can thrive today.

6ddd07c1930cb2643e2382b12201da89.description: A summit designed for sharpening UX research skills and getting inspired by real-world case studies.

925bbef774410951ba34ce336be893de.location: Atlanta Marriott NE Hotel, Atlanta, Georgia
925bbef774410951ba34ce336be893de.description: Transforming Government through Accelerating Impact and Modernizing Technology. Technology has unlocked countless opportunities for growth, collaboration, and improvement among the State of Georgia’s agencies, departments & beyond. This one-day, in-person program will convene public sector leaders to discuss modernizing platforms, leveraging data and analytics, and the evolution of technology, data, digital transformation, AI, and the workforce, providing an ideal space for networking and sharing best practices.

83d53c131db5263a46fa3f66d76cbebb.description: A programme designed for researchers, hosted at the Young Entrepreneurs in Science learning space by the Falling Walls Foundation. The workshop runs from 9 am-6 pm. Spots are limited to approximately 25 participants and allocated on a first-come, first-served basis. Selected participants will receive a confirmation email with further details.

37f60a5e43871f4502def3fd61aba035.description: 5-day 'Executive School' with a deeper emphasis on facilitation skills, project planning, and embedding service design in strategy and everyday business. Covers core tools and processes to start your own projects.
